curation.synopsis: Exiled to the rotting jungle world of Sedh for crimes against his own court, the necron lord Oltyx commands a dwindling garrison against an endless tide of ork invaders, gnawed by bitterness over the dynasty he once stood to inherit. When the orks' assaults take on a desperate cast and a far worse predator begins to drift through the canopy, Oltyx realises Sedh is only the first stone falling. Returning to the Ithakas crownworld means crossing the brother who exiled him, a court rotted through with denial, and a horror tangled with the buried origin of the necrons themselves. Nate Crowley's debut Necron novel reads as a tragedy of memory: an immortal warrior whose mind is fraying piece by piece, fighting to remember why his species ever chose this kind of life.
